challenges.title: Void Elements
challenges.description: Create HTML that uses at least 4 different void elements: br, hr, img (with src and alt), input, and meta. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Use at least 4 different void elements -->
<!-- Void elements: br, hr, img, input, meta -->

"""
challenges.test_code: assert ' element' assert ' element' assert ' element' assert 'src=' in html.lower(), 'The img element should have a src attribute' assert 'alt=' in html.lower(), 'The img element should have an alt attribute' assert ' element' assert ' element'
hints: Void elements cannot have content and do not need a closing tag
The <meta> element goes in the head, but for this challenge you can include it anywhere

challenges.title: Self-Closing Gallery
challenges.description: Create a page that uses void elements in context: at least 2 img elements (with src and alt), br for line breaks, hr as a separator, an input for search, a link element for a stylesheet, and a meta element for charset. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a page using void elements in context -->
<!-- Include img, br, hr, input, link, and meta elements -->

"""
challenges.test_code: assert html.lower().count('= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 img elements' assert html.lower().count('alt=') >= 2, 'Each img should have an alt attribute' assert ' element' assert ' element' assert ' element' assert ' element' assert 'rel=' in html.lower(), 'The link element should have a rel attribute' assert ' element' assert 'charset=' in html.lower(), 'The meta element should specify a charset'
hints: Use <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css"> for the stylesheet
Use <meta charset="utf-8"> for the charset declaration
Place images with and separate them with
